skill.name: pair-programming
skill.description: Guides AI-assisted pair programming sessions with Claude Flow commands for driver, navigator, switch, TDD, review, mentor, and debug workflows.
skill.summary: Run structured AI pair programming sessions with roles, verification, testing, and review workflows.
skill.author: ruvnet

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many external command patterns because SKILL.md is a Markdown guide with shell examples for the claude-flow CLI. These commands are legitimate for the stated pair-programming purpose, but they can modify repositories, import configuration, export session data, and run auto-commit workflows. The weak cryptography and reconnaissance alerts are false positives from prose, headings, route examples, and troubleshooting text; no prompt injection attempt or malicious exfiltration pattern was found.

medium_findings.title: External CLI Commands Can Modify Local Projects
medium_findings.description: The skill is a command guide that asks users to install and run the claude-flow CLI, start sessions, import configuration, export reports, and enable auto-commit or CI modes. This is aligned with the skill purpose, but users should review commands before execution because they can change repository state or session files.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file contains direct shell command examples for a local CLI and options that can affect repository workflow. The commands are not hidden or malicious, but the operational impact is clear.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern
low_findings.description: The static weak cryptography alerts point to prose, headings, command descriptions, JSON configuration examples, and API route examples. No weak hash function, cipher use, credential handling, or cryptographic implementation was found at the cited locations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Reviewed context shows ordinary documentation text and examples, not cryptographic code. The repeated scanner matches appear to come from substrings or generic security wording.

low_findings.title: False Positive: System Reconnaissance Pattern
low_findings.description: The reconnaissance alerts reference ordinary documentation such as rapid prototyping text, REST endpoint examples, mode descriptions, and troubleshooting advice to check system resources. No command enumerates host details, collects environment data, or exfiltrates information.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are visible documentation examples with no reconnaissance commands or collection logic. One troubleshooting line mentions system resources, but it does not instruct sensitive enumeration.

dangerous_patterns.title: Documented External Command Execution
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill relies on users running local shell commands for the claude-flow CLI. This is expected for a development workflow skill, but marketplace users should treat the commands as privileged local actions.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The commands are explicit Markdown examples rather than concealed execution. The risk comes from user-driven local command execution, not from hidden code in the skill.

content.value_statement: Developers need structured AI collaboration that supports coding, review, testing, and learning. This skill provides Claude Flow workflows for pair programming sessions with roles, modes, verification, and session management.
actual_capabilities: Explains driver, navigator, switch, TDD, review, mentor, and debug modes.
Provides Claude Flow CLI commands for starting, pausing, resuming, and ending sessions.
Describes workflows for testing, coverage checks, code review, debugging, and performance review.
Shows configuration patterns for profiles, agents, shortcuts, and session behavior.
Documents session persistence features such as save, load, export, replay, and reporting.
Lists quality thresholds for truth score, coverage, and complexity.
limitations: Requires the separate Claude Flow CLI and a local development environment.
Does not include executable helper scripts or automated enforcement inside the skill package.
Command behavior depends on the installed Claude Flow version and project configuration.
Users must review commands before running options that affect files, commits, or exports.

use_cases.title: Feature Delivery With Shared Roles
use_cases.description: Use switch mode to alternate implementation and review while building a feature with tests and verification.

use_cases.title: Learning Through Guided Coding
use_cases.description: Use mentor mode to get explanations, examples, and slower-paced guidance while writing code.

use_cases.title: Focused Debugging Sessions
use_cases.description: Use debug mode and session reports to isolate defects, add regression tests, and document root causes.

prompt_templates.title: Start a Basic Session
prompt_templates.prompt: Start a pair programming session for this repository. Use driver mode and explain each suggested step before I run commands.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a TDD Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me run a TDD pair programming session for this feature. Define the first failing tests, implementation steps, and review checkpoints.

prompt_templates.title: Review and Refactor Safely
prompt_templates.prompt: Guide a review-mode session for this module. Focus on security, performance, test coverage, and small refactoring steps.

prompt_templates.title: Coordinate an Advanced Session
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a switch-mode pair programming session with roles, verification thresholds, session notes, export requirements, and commit review gates.

output_examples.input: Start a pair programming session for a login feature.
output_examples.output: A structured session plan with recommended mode, role responsibilities, first implementation target, testing checkpoints, and review criteria.

output_examples.input: Help debug a failing API test.
output_examples.output: A debugging workflow that narrows the failure, checks assumptions, adds a regression test, and records the root cause.

output_examples.input: Prepare a refactoring session for a complex component.
output_examples.output: A refactoring plan with scope boundaries, verification steps, risk checks, and a summary format for session notes.

best_practices: Use a clean git workspace before running commands that modify files or commits.
Choose the session mode based on the work type, such as TDD for new behavior or review mode for quality checks.
Run tests and review generated changes before saving, exporting, or committing session results.
anti_patterns: Running command examples without checking their effect on the current repository.
Using auto-commit or CI options before reviewing generated code and tests.
Treating truth scores or coverage thresholds as substitutes for human review.

faq.question: Does this skill write code by itself?
faq.answer: No. It provides workflows and commands for Claude Flow pair programming sessions. The installed CLI performs the active workflow.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Do I need Claude Flow installed?
faq.answer: Yes. The documented workflows require the Claude Flow CLI in the local development environment.

faq.question: Can this help with test driven development?
faq.answer: Yes. It documents TDD mode, test generation, coverage checks, and test-first session patterns.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes, with a medium risk warning. The risk comes from documented external commands, not confirmed malicious content.

faq.question: What should users review before running it?
faq.answer: Users should review commands that install packages, import configuration, export session data, or create commits.
